cases.synopsis: Complaint by TE James Serio et al. against Palmetto Presbytery concerning a candidate's examination on charismatic gifts was found in order and referred to a commission for adjudication. No full case record or opinion appears in this window.

cases.title: Constitutional Inquiry #1 from Central Carolina Presbytery (extraordinary clause for ordination)
cases.synopsis: Central Carolina Presbytery inquired about the scope of the extraordinary clause in BCO 18-2 for ordination, including whether presbytery may waive the six-month endorsement, the two-month filing deadline, the internship sequence, and whether college and seminary diplomas are pre-requisites versus trial parts. The committee answered each sub-question, concluding that the three educational pre-requisites in BCO 21-4 are not parts of the trial for ordination and cannot be waived under the extraordinary clause.

cases.title: Constitutional Inquiry #2 from Covenant Presbyterian Church, Palm Bay, Florida (election of officers)

parties.complainant_or_appellant: Covenant Presbyterian Church, Palm Bay, Florida

cases.synopsis: Covenant Presbyterian Church asked whether a session may limit the ballot to two of four qualified nominees to control board size. The committee answered no: BCO 24-1 requires the session to report all eligible men to the congregation after examination, and the congregation determines the number to be elected.

cases.title: Constitutional Inquiry #3 from Presbytery of the Ascension (resignation of minister from non-PCA pastoral charge)
cases.synopsis: The Presbytery of the Ascension asked whether BCO 23-1 applies to pastoral charges outside PCA jurisdiction, such as an independent church. The committee answered no: Chapter 23 does not apply to churches not under the jurisdiction of the Presbytery.

cases.title: Constitutional Inquiry #4 from Presbytery of the Ascension (dissolution of congregation and membership roll)
cases.synopsis: The Presbytery of the Ascension raised five questions about what happens to the session, corporation, and members upon dissolution of a particular church. The committee answered each question and noted that BCO does not clearly address all these matters, suggesting the presbytery might bring overtures to the assembly.

cases.title: Constitutional Inquiry #5 from Stony Point Reformed Presbyterian Church (removal of members without judicial process)
cases.synopsis: Stony Point Reformed Presbyterian Church asked whether a letter requesting to be dropped from the roll constitutes evidence of no intention to fulfill church vows, and what 'delete' in BCO 46-5 means other than excommunication. The committee referred to prior GA answers on the matter and noted that Overture 5 before the assembly might address the issue.

cases.title: Constitutional Inquiry #6 from Town North Presbyterian Church, Richardson, TX (rotation system for elders and deacons)

parties.complainant_or_appellant: Town North Presbyterian Church, Richardson, TX

cases.synopsis: Town North Presbyterian Church asked whether a session may preclude a previously ordained officer from being nominated due to unacceptability (not heresy or immorality), and whether an inactive elder or deacon must be subject to BCO 38-2 or 34-10. The committee answered that all qualified nominees must be presented for election and that an inactive officer is not subject to either provision.

cases.title: Constitutional Inquiry #7 from Eastern Carolina Presbytery (belief in infant baptism and limited atonement as requirement for office)
cases.synopsis: Eastern Carolina Presbytery inquired about the necessity of belief in infant baptism and limited atonement to be an officer of the church. The committee declined to answer because there was no matter pending before the lower court as required by BCO 41-1.

cases.title: Constitutional Inquiry #8 from Western Carolina Presbytery (definite and indefinite suspension)
cases.synopsis: Western Carolina Presbytery requested advice concerning the application of definite and indefinite suspension. The committee declined to answer because the matter was directly related to pending judicial cases from Western Carolina Presbytery.

cases.title: Constitutional Inquiry #9 from Town North Presbyterian Church, Richardson, TX (further inquiry on rotation system)

parties.complainant_or_appellant: Town North Presbyterian Church, Richardson, TX

cases.synopsis: Town North Presbyterian Church submitted a further inquiry on the rotation system for elders and deacons. The committee referred items 1 and 2 to its previous answer on Constitutional Inquiry #6 and declined to answer item 3 due to insufficient information about the church's intent and circumstances.
